growth_records.medium: In silico nutrient-rich minimal medium, fructose sole carbon source

up.method: imposed FBA uptake bound (sole carbon source)

growth_records.cite: Foster et al. Modeling Growth Kinetics, Interspecies Cell Fusion, and Metabolism of a Clostridium acetobutylicum/Clostridium ljungdahlii Syntrophic Coculture. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.01325-20. [PMC8573953]
growth_records.snippet: Clj nonhybrid (iJL680) 539 453 iJL680 0.22 ... maximum growth rate using 5 mmol gDW−1 h−1 fructose as the sole carbon source
growth_records.notes: Model (FBA) MAXIMUM growth rate, not experimental: iJL680 gave mu=0.22 h-1 with imposed fructose uptake 5 mmol/gDW/h (sole carbon). doubling_time = ln2/0.22 = 3.15 h (calculated). Hybrid variant gave 0.24 h-1. Note after 15 h in coculture the Cac growth rate was reported an order of magnitude lower than that of Clj. Low confidence: theoretical GEM output.

growth_records.strain: Engineered IPA-producing strain (overexpressing acetoacetyl-CoA transferase AACT and acetoacetate decarboxylase AADC; strain created by Lo et al., ref 17)
growth_records.cite: Zhou, Guangye et al. Bioadaptive Ni single atoms unlock high rate microbial electrosynthesis of isopropanol from CO2. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-68358-8. [PMC12905360]
growth_records.snippet: CO-supplemented fermentation produced ~11.4 mM IPA (681.3 mg/L), representing a 142-times enhancement, which was also accompanied by a 1.7 times (73.9 mM, 4359.3 mg/L) increase in acetate and a 3.3 times boost (29.3 mM, 1347.8 mg/L) in etha
growth_records.notes: Batch gas fermentation, CO/H2/CO2 (50/20/30) feeding, 37 C, YT medium. Values are FINAL titers (not gDW-normalized specific rates). Titers reported both in mM and mg/L in text. No growth rate given: authors state OD600 became unreliable because dead cells remained in suspension after day 3-5; only CFU/mL monitored, no numeric mu reported. Gas substrate (CO/H2/CO2) consumption not quantified, so uptake_rates left empty. medium composition from Methods: 10 g/L Bacto yeast extract, 16 g/L Bacto tryptone, 4 g/L NaCl, 0.5 g/L cysteine-HCl; 5 g/L fructose was added ONLY when reviving frozen stocks (not in fermentation medium). pH from Methods/text: bioelectrolyte pH approx 6.

growth_records.strain: Engineered IPA-producing strain (overexpressing AACT and AADC; from Lo et al., ref 17)
growth_records.medium: YT bioelectrolyte (integrated microbial electrochemical system, iMES)

sec.method: GC/HPLC of bioelectrolyte, daily sampling over 4-day run

growth_records.cite: Zhou, Guangye et al. Bioadaptive Ni single atoms unlock high rate microbial electrosynthesis of isopropanol from CO2. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-68358-8. [PMC12905360]
growth_records.snippet: The hybrid iMES system achieved an IPA production rate of 161.3 mg/L/day (10.8 A/m2) under ambient conditions ... we achieved the CO-mediated CO2-to-IPA microbial electrochemical synthesis with an average rate of 161.3 mg/L/day
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition: CO2 microbial electrosynthesis (iMES biohybrid) rather than bottled syngas fermentation; CO donor generated electrochemically in situ. IPA volumetric productivity 161.3 mg/L/day at 10.8 A/m2, -0.75 V vs RHE, 37 C, 4-day operation, initial OD600 ~0.5. Volumetric (per-litre) rate, not gDW-normalized; no biomass-specific rate or growth rate reported. Bioelectrolyte = YT medium; ion chromatography gave 93.3 mM Na+ and 13.3 mM K+ (Supplementary Table S1). Note a cited comparison (ref 64) reports ~116.7 mg/L/day PHB with ~100 mg/L/day IPA byproduct using a different strain/pathway (NOT this organism).

growth_records.strain: IPA v3-2 (engineered isopropanol-producing strain; IPA pathway integrated at pyrE locus)
growth_records.agit: 250 rpm (seed cultures); bioreactor 300 rpm increased gradually to 900 rpm
growth_records.medium: YTF medium + 20 g/L fructose, supplemented with syngas (mixotrophic)

up.units: g/L (supplied concentration; consumption rate not quantified)

yields.product_yields: IPA became the equivalently largest carbon sink; acetate the major product; carbon-molar partitioning reported in Fig. 5

growth_records.cite: Lo, Jonathan et al. Thermodynamic and Kinetic Modeling Directs Pathway Optimization for Isopropanol Production in a Gas-Fermenting Bacterium. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01274-22. [PMC10134883]
growth_records.method: reported OD600 only (peak OD600 = 3.48 at day 3); no specific growth rate reported and none derivable without OD-to-gDW conversion
growth_records.snippet: mixotrophic growth ... highest optical density of 3.48 achieved at day 3 ... final IPA titers ... 74 ± 1.3 mM (4.44 ± 0.078 g/L). Acetate was the major product, which reached 117 mM, after 7 days. Both ethanol and 3-hydroxybutyrate (3HB) ..
growth_records.notes: Engineered gas-fermenting acetogen. Products are HPLC-measured end-point TITERS (mM / g/L), not specific rates in mmol/gDW/h; biomass reported only as OD600 (peak 3.48 at day 3) with no OD-to-gDW factor, so per-biomass rates are not derivable. All secreted products (IPA, acetate, ethanol, 3HB) are experimental. Fructose (20 g/L) and syngas are supplied substrates but consumption rates not quantified (is_experimental=false as rates). Earlier construct titers reported in same paper (context, not separate records): IPA v1 0.78 g/L IPA + 0.05 g/L acetone; IPA v2 0.97 g/L; ctfAB+aadc 2.7 g/L; atoAD+aadc 1.7 g/L in YTF. [metadata completed from paper Methods: pH]

growth_records.agit: 250 rpm (seed cultures); bioreactor 300 rpm increased gradually to 900 rpm
growth_records.medium: YT medium (yeast extract + tryptone, no fructose), autotrophic syngas fermentation

up.units: 70%/20%/10% v/v at 300 sccm; uptake not quantified

sec.units: mM (peak titer = 2.39 g/L at day 12; 29% carbon molarity)
mM (max at end of fermentation; 34% carbon molarity)

yields.product_yields: IPA up to 29% of product carbon molarity (C-Proportion_max); acetate 34%; carbon-molar yields over time in Fig. 5

growth_records.cite: Lo, Jonathan et al. Thermodynamic and Kinetic Modeling Directs Pathway Optimization for Isopropanol Production in a Gas-Fermenting Bacterium. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01274-22. [PMC10134883]
growth_records.method: reported OD600 only (peak OD600 ~4-5 at day 13); no specific growth rate reported
growth_records.snippet: grew exponentially, reaching the highest optical density (OD) of around 4 to 5 at day 13 ... IPA production reached the maximum at day 12 ... peak titer (Titer_max) of 39.7 mM (2.39 g/L) ... acetate ... reached its highest level (80.1 mM; C
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic (gas-only) 2-L bioreactor run of same engineered strain. HPLC end-point titers (mM / g/L), not per-biomass rates; biomass only as OD600 (~4-5 at day 13), no gDW conversion so rates not derivable. All secreted products experimental. Syngas uptake not quantified. pH controlled at 5.2, 37°C, 300 sccm gas sparge. Carbon partitioning: IPA 29%, acetate 34% of product carbon.

growth_records.medium: Modified DSM 879 medium (NaHCO3 omitted; 50 mM MES buffer)

up.method: measured (HPLC fructose depletion) constrained in FBA
FBA-derived (NOT directly measured) for this culture

sec.units: measured by HPLC (Dionex Ultimate 3000); numeric rate not stated in text
measured by HPLC; numeric rate not in text

growth_records.cite: Caivano, Antonio et al. Enzyme-constrained metabolic model and in silico metabolic engineering of Clostridium ljungdahlii for the development of sustainable production processes. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.csbj.2023.09.015. [PMC10543971]
growth_records.snippet: Fructose, CO2 -0.672, -15.861 0.110 [This study] ... C. ljungdahlii mixotrophic cultivations were carried out to retrieve data about growth rate, uptake, and secretion fluxes during simultaneous CO2, H2, and fructose consumption
growth_records.notes: Mixotrophic experimental culture #1 (this study, Table 2). Measured fructose uptake = 0.672 mmol/gCDW/h, CO2 uptake = 15.861 mmol/gCDW/h, growth rate = 0.110 h-1. Doubling = ln2/0.110 = 6.30 h (calculated). H2 uptake 33.6 mmol/gCDW/h is FBA-derived per text ('value obtained from previous FBA'), so flagged is_experimental:false. Acetate/ethanol secretion measured by HPLC but numeric rates not given in article text. Fructose tested at 5 and 10 g/L; specific bottle conc for this row not pinned. pH monitored but value not stated in text.

growth_records.medium: Modified DSM 879 medium (NaHCO3 omitted; 50 mM MES)
growth_records.cite: Caivano, Antonio et al. Enzyme-constrained metabolic model and in silico metabolic engineering of Clostridium ljungdahlii for the development of sustainable production processes. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.csbj.2023.09.015. [PMC10543971]
growth_records.notes: Mixotrophic experimental culture #2 (this study, Table 2). Measured fructose uptake 0.615, CO2 uptake 16.480 mmol/gCDW/h, growth 0.102 h-1. Doubling = ln2/0.102 = 6.80 h (calculated). Anaerobic, 37 C, modified DSM 879.

Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal (2023). DOI:10.1016/j.csbj.2023.09.015. [PMC10543971]
growth_records.notes: Mixotrophic experimental culture #3 (this study, Table 2). Measured fructose uptake 0.507, CO2 uptake 8.785 mmol/gCDW/h, growth 0.064 h-1. Doubling = ln2/0.064 = 10.83 h (calculated). Note: Table 2 also lists literature-cited experimental rates for C. ljungdahlii NOT from this study - CO+H2 (H2 -7.724, CO -40.132; mu 0.043) ref [11]; H2 -79.5, mu 0.033 ref [12]; H2+CO2 (-46.630, -21.765; mu 0.024) ref [11]; CO -38.5, mu 0.058 ref [12]; CO -35.878, mu 0.060 ref [11]; fructose -1.88, mu 0.72 ref [29]. These are cited from other papers, not curated as this paper's records.
